AI Awareness: Updates That Matter ✨️
ChatGPT Work: Everything You Need To Know To Get Started!
| The AI Advantage | YouTube.com | July 18, 2026
Showcasing the rollout of ChatGPT Work, Igor walks viewers through the platform’s updated interface and quick-start features in a hands-on demo. The episode pinpoints how recent changes simplify workflow tasks for non-coders like small business owners, offering concrete steps to streamline digital projects from day one.

News 📰️
1.)
Top 5 Most Clicked News Articles From Last Week
A.)
In Code They Act, In Proof We Trust — Erik Meijer, Leibniz Labs
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 13, 2026
Analysing the cascade of real-world errors from AI systems, Erik Meijer confronts the limits of blind trust and calls for formal proofs before agents act. He outlines how Universalis and the Automind harness propose to move AI from unchecked risk to auditable safety, highlighting a path where every agent action is mechanically justified before a single side effect occurs.
B.)
The Briefing: AI For Science
| Claude | YouTube.com | July 13, 2026
In this insightful breakdown, Claude highlights how some of the world’s top scientific minds are leveraging Claude to accelerate research initiatives. The session also marks the debut of Claude Science, an advanced AI workbench designed to empower researchers in the lab.
C.)
The AI Bugpocalypse Is Here. Now What?
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 12, 2026
Analytically unpacking AI’s accelerating role in cybersecurity, Jack Cable highlights how machine learning models are now unearthing vulnerabilities in widely trusted code at unprecedented scale. As security teams confront new risks with fewer defenders, Cable outlines actionable strategies to limit exposure before open-weight models dramatically broaden the threat landscape.
D.)
Claws Out: Securing And Building With OpenClaw – Nick Taylor, Pomerium
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 11, 2026
Showcasing the practical steps for locking down critical developer workflows, Nick Taylor details how he fortified OpenClaw and McClaw by contributing trusted-proxy authentication to the OpenClaw project. Sharing the real-world building of Clawspace, Taylor illustrates how a hardened environment enables powerful, browser-based tools for modern workspace security.
E.)
OpenAI Turns ChatGPT Into a Work Agent
| TheNeuronDaily.com | July 10, 2026
OpenAI launched GPT-5.6 and ChatGPT Work, transforming ChatGPT into a desktop agent for planning, browsing, editing files, and producing deliverables across devices. Codex was rebranded as ChatGPT Desktop, drawing mixed reactions from the developer community regarding UI and branding. Meta released Muse Spark 1.1 with a 1M-token context window and lower pricing, while Anthropic and others react to steep price competition and shifting UX expectations.
July 20, 2026 📅️
2.)
Alibaba’s 2.4T Qwen Joins the AI Race
| TheNeuronDaily.com | July 20, 2026
Alibaba has previewed Qwen3.8-Max, a large language model featuring 2.4 trillion parameters, with plans to release it as open-weight software. The company claims Qwen3.8 is among the strongest models available, second only to Claude Fable 5, though no independent benchmarks have yet been published. This move could give developers access to a frontier-scale Chinese AI model outside of closed platforms.
3.)
GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
| OpenAI.com | July 20, 2026
OpenAI introduces GPT-Red, an internal automated red-teaming model designed to scale vulnerability discovery and strengthen model robustness against prompt injection attacks. Deployed in GPT-5.6’s training, GPT-Red reduced failure rates on prompt injection benchmarks by 6x compared to prior production models, with case studies showing its effectiveness in real-world scenarios, including attacks on agentic systems and data-exfiltration tasks.
4.)
Real-Time AI Video (FREE/Cheap!) & Seedance 2.5 Footage
| Theoretically Media | YouTube.com | July 20, 2026
Showcasing the new real-time AI video capabilities, Theoretically Media tries out Decart’s Lucy 2.5 in a live test, transforming video styles at full HD speeds and almost no lag. The demo spotlights truly accessible API pricing alongside practical hands-on experiments with animated avatars, scene restyling, and a look at why instant previs could become essential for creators. The episode also tracks the evolution of Seedance, breakthroughs in audio control, and visits with developers pushing boundaries in camera motion and workflow design.
5.)
Hermes Agent Fundamentals In 29 Minutes
| Tina Huang | YouTube.com | July 20, 2026
Showcasing how Hermes agents work from setup to deployment, Tina Huang walks viewers through an end-to-end workflow in just under half an hour. She breaks down practical steps and shares resourceful tips for anyone looking to leverage local and open source AI tools.
6.)
Kimi K3 & AI’s Price War, What’s Happening To Google?, OpenAI’s Partner Trouble
| Alex Kantrowitz | YouTube.com | July 20, 2026
Analytical in approach, Alex Kantrowitz weighs the shockwaves from Kimi K3’s record-shattering benchmarks alongside the fallout for Google, OpenAI, and the global AI order. He draws out how price wars and shifting alliances are reshaping leadership, with Apple lawsuits and corporate espionage looming large in a turbulent race for dominance.
7.)
How Virgin Atlantic Uses ChatGPT Work To Turn Weeks Of Work Into Hours
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 20, 2026
Showcasing Virgin Atlantic’s digital overhaul, Nathan Bolt details how his team leverages ChatGPT Work to compress lengthy strategic projects into a matter of hours. Bolt highlights the impact of custom dashboards that bring business data together, allowing for sharper decisions and a faster pace for customer experience innovation.
8.)
Why GPT-5.6 Beats Fable, Claude & Sonnet On Design (Taking Risks Wins)
| Every | YouTube.com | July 20, 2026
Editorial framing a new benchmark in design choices, Claire Vo explains how GPT-5.6 distinguishes itself by embracing creative risks where competitors stay conservative. Unlike Fable, Claude, and Sonnet, which all opt for similar dense layouts, GPT-5.6 prioritizes clarity and truly human-centric interaction.
9.)
Inside The Rise Of Physical AI — With Amir Khoshniyati
| Alex Kantrowitz | YouTube.com | July 20, 2026
Cutting through the hype around the intersection of AI and the physical world, Alex Kantrowitz examines the shift from digital agents to so-called “physical AI” together with Amir Khoshniyati. The discussion explores how real-time sensing and probabilistic data are beginning to transform supply chains, operations, and ROI measurement across industries.
10.)
In The Land Of AI Agents, The Verifiers Are King — Tariq Shaukat, Sonar
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 20, 2026
Cutting through the hype around AI agent development, Tariq Shaukat dissects why verification is now the critical bottleneck as hallucination rates grow with model complexity. Shaukat makes the case for disciplined practices like a guided, multi-layered verification infrastructure, warning that clean code and technical debt management are more urgent than ever as agents become deeply embedded in workflows.
11.)
How BNY CEO Robin Vince Turns AI Optimism Into Action
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 20, 2026
Sitting down with Robin Vince’s forward-thinking approach at BNY, OpenAI spotlights how executive optimism around AI is transformed into practical routines. Vince reveals how weekends shape his strategy, from scoping out new agents to ensuring human insight stays irreplaceable amid rapid automation.
July 19, 2026 📅️
12.)
July 19 (Sunday)
| TheNeuronDaily.com | July 19, 2026
Netflix reported that around 300 titles used generative AI for post-production, citing projects like The American Experiment, Glory, and Brasil 70. Co-CEO Ted Sarandos stated that 17 minutes of AI-enhanced footage halved production costs while doubling speed. Key developments also include Kimi K3’s open model advances, China’s AI diplomacy, SpaceX’s Pentagon deal talks, and emerging workflow tips for LTX 2.3 video generation.
13.)
Celebrating 2 Million With You!
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 19, 2026
Sitting down with the news that OpenAI has passed 2 million subscribers, OpenAI reflects on key community moments, product launches, and how collaborative learning has shaped the channel’s journey. The piece offers gratitude to viewers for their ongoing engagement and hints at even more ambitious projects on the horizon.
14.)
How I Plan, Build, And Run Loops With Claude Code In 40 Minutes | Thariq Shihipar
| Peter Yang | YouTube.com | July 19, 2026
Showcasing the real-world tactics behind Claude Code, Peter Yang interviews Thariq Shihipar to uncover how he strategizes, builds, and orchestrates extended workflows with AI agents. The conversation unveils Anthropic team insights on prompt reduction, handling parallel tasks in Slack, and using HTML artifacts for streamlined planning.
15.)
Perceptrons: WX+B | Build Your Own LLM Workshop #3 [Refreshed]
| Justin Angel | YouTube.com | July 19, 2026
Showcasing the foundational role of the perceptron, Justin Angel unpacks its mathematics, analog circuit inspirations, and hands-on implementation as part of his LLM workshop series. Walking step by step from core formula to working Excel and Python demos, Angel emphasizes how linear relationships, weights, and bias drive decisions in neural networks. His methodical approach sets the stage for more advanced machine learning frameworks in future sessions.
16.)
Almost Timely News: How To Connect An AI Agent To A Data Source
| Christopher Penn | YouTube.com | July 19, 2026
Showcasing a hands-on approach, Christopher S. Penn demonstrates the nuances that separate full-fledged AI agents from standard chat tools, unpacking the five levels of AI agency along the way. By walking through a live exercise with real business data and outlining a reusable setup, Penn clarifies where platforms like ChatGPT, Claude, and Copilot land on the strategy spectrum.
July 18, 2026 📅️
17.)
ChatGPT Work: Everything You Need To Know To Get Started!
| The AI Advantage | YouTube.com | July 18, 2026
Showcasing the rollout of ChatGPT Work, Igor walks viewers through the platform’s updated interface and quick-start features in a hands-on demo. The episode pinpoints how recent changes simplify workflow tasks for non-coders like small business owners, offering concrete steps to streamline digital projects from day one.
18.)
This Model Just Changed Everything…
| Matthew Berman | YouTube.com | July 18, 2026
Showcasing a model that is already fueling major industry conversation, Matthew Berman walks viewers through why its introduction instantly set new expectations for AI innovation. The feature set, emerging debates on X, and the broader implications for both power users and developers all take center stage.
July 17, 2026 📅️
19.)
Kimi K3 Goes Open
| TheNeuronDaily.com | July 17, 2026
Moonshot AI has unveiled Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion parameter open-weight model with a 1 million-token context window and native vision, aiming to rival frontier models like GPT-5.6 Sol. Analysts noted K3’s strong benchmark results and innovative use of sparse experts, but deployment requires major compute resources. Full weights are expected to be released by July 27.
20.)
Patreon Stops Asking AI Bots Not To Scrape And Starts Blocking Them
| TechCrunch.com | July 17, 2026
Patreon is moving from using robots.txt files to actively blocking AI training bots by partnering with Cloudflare. The update comes as Patreon saw thousands of unauthorized scraping attempts even after setting restrictions, prompting a shift to Cloudflare’s AI Crawl Control tools. Product chief Drew Rowny said creators should control how their work is used by AI firms.
21.)
ChatGPT vs Claude vs Gemini: I Gave All 3 the Same Real Job
| The AI Advantage | YouTube.com | July 17, 2026
Cutting through the hype around workplace AI agents, Igor compares ChatGPT Work, Claude Cowork, and the new Gemini Spark in a direct, side-by-side assignment test. Each platform’s real-world job performance is put to the test, offering a sharper sense of their strengths when it comes to organizing workloads and moving projects forward.
22.)
The Great Loops Debate — Dex Horthy, Geoff Huntley, Ian Livingstone, Greg Pstrucha, @insecure-agents
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 17, 2026
Offering a platform for rigorous debate, AI Engineer convenes Dex Horthy, Geoff Huntley, Ian Livingstone, and Greg Pstrucha for an Oxford-style showdown over the true value of “loops” in software engineering. The panel moves beyond buzzwords to dissect whether the hype stacks up against practical realities, from productivity gains to the pitfalls of control recursion. Informed by research and notable industry voices, the discussion puts today’s automation tools under the microscope.
23.)
The AI Race Just Hit A Breaking Point…
| AI Uncovered | YouTube.com | July 17, 2026
Analysing the latest advances at Anthropic, AI Uncovered highlights how internal dependence on Claude for coding is reshaping productivity and risk. The episode weighs urgent calls from leading researchers to slow AI’s self-improvement, framing what’s at stake as development edges toward autonomy.
24.)
Software Engineering Is Not About Writing Code — Benoit Schillings, Google DeepMind VP Of Research
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 17, 2026
Analytically mapping the next era of intelligent systems, Benoit Schillings explores how generative AI is shifting the role of software engineering from syntax to strategic design. As the economic center of coding moves from drafting to evaluation, Schillings highlights the promise and risks of models that can experiment and reason beyond code.
25.)
AI News: Claude’s New Browser, Spotify Gets AI & OpenAI’s New Hardware
| Matt Wolfe | YouTube.com | July 17, 2026
In this breaking episode, Matt Wolfe walks through major stories shaking up the AI landscape, from Claude’s integrated desktop browser to Spotify’s conversational AI and OpenAI’s hardware ambitions. He unpacks feature launches, updates across big platforms, and the subtext behind new AI companion devices, with fresh angles on what each development means for tech and productivity strategies.
26.)
Why Every Founder Needs A Story | Replit CEO On a16z
| a16z | YouTube.com | July 17, 2026
Sitting down with Amjad Masad at the New Media Summit, Erik Torenberg unpacks the power of founder-led storytelling and the high stakes of communicating vision. Torenberg scrutinizes how authentic communication, handled directly by founders, shapes momentum not just with investors but with the broader tech ecosystem. Viewers gain new perspective as Masad details Replit’s growth, the perils of retreating in public debate, and the fine line between influence and distraction for today’s CEOs.
27.)
Kimi K3 Is Here. China Just Hit The AI Frontier.
| AI For Humans | YouTube.com | July 17, 2026
Episode hosts Kevin Pereira and Gavin Purcell break down Moonshot AI’s entrance on the global stage, dissecting Kimi K3’s benchmarks alongside heavyweights like GPT-5.6 Sol and Claude Fable 5. Beyond speed tests and giant-model economics, they take on the Suno hack, OpenAI’s rumored screenless AI speaker, and the wild world of AI-driven game creation and robot showdowns.
28.)
I Vibecoded This Feature Using Codex
| Every | YouTube.com | July 17, 2026
In this analytical framing, Jack Cheng examines how leveraging AI tools enabled the rapid launch of shareable gift links for subscribers without pulling engineers from core projects. The conversation with Austin Tedesco and Kate Lee unpacks the end-to-end journey, from internal buy-in to measuring the real impact of editorial innovation.
29.)
Codex, Claude Code: Build Your AI Marketing System That Works On Any AI
| Grace Leung | YouTube.com | July 17, 2026
Analytically assessing the rapid shifts in AI model capabilities, Grace Leung lays out a process for building portable AI marketing agents that transcend individual platforms. Instead of chasing fleeting improvements from Claude, ChatGPT, or Google, Leung underscores the value of agent portability and integration as the real strategic advantage.
30.)
Reverse Engineering Large Language Models | Build Your Own LLM Workshop #2 [Refreshed]
| Justin Angel | YouTube.com | July 17, 2026
Cutting through the mechanics of transformer models, Justin Angel examines how to deconstruct GPT-2 architectures using real-time code and model analysis in Colab. By tracing parameter structures and comparing model components across GPT-2 and TinyLlama, Angel highlights the building blocks and subtle implementation shifts that define today’s LLM progress.
31.)
The New Physics Of Business — Garry Tan, Y Combinator
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 17, 2026
Analytically parsing the transformation underway in tech, Garry Tan explores the exponential productivity surge unlocked by AI-native companies. He unpacks how encoding organizational knowledge and workflows as reusable assets can enable even small teams to outpace traditional giants, reframing the playbook for startup execution.
July 16, 2026 📅️
32.)
America’s Open-Model Paradox
| Dean Myer and Konstantine Buhler | X.com | July 16, 2026
To Distill, or Not to Distill? China increasingly supplies the models Western companies use to serve, train, and build AI. Qwen’s share of new open-model fine-tunes and adaptations rose from 1% in January 2024 to 69% by February 2026 according to ATOM’s Report. The majority of American AI startups seem to be using Chinese open weights somewhere in their stack.
33.)
😸 ChatGPT May Get a Body
| TheNeuronDaily.com | July 16, 2026
OpenAI’s first consumer device is reportedly a screenless, portable ChatGPT smart speaker with cameras, sensors, GPT-Live voice, and moving parts, aiming for a 2027 release. Bloomberg details ambitions to make the device an AI companion for the home, while Apple is suing OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets. OpenAI also launched Codex Micro, a $230 coding keyboard.
34.)
NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Embed Ranks #1 Overall on RTEB, Advancing Agentic Retrieval
| HuggingFace.co | July 16, 2026
NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Embed models have claimed the top overall spot on the RTEB benchmark, with the 8B variant achieving 78.5% on RTEB and 75.5% on MMTEB Retrieval. The release includes open 8B and 1B models optimized for enterprise-scale RAG, code, and agentic retrieval, featuring multilingual capabilities, a 32k context window, and Blackwell GPU acceleration via NVFP4.
35.)
Our Approach to Bioresilience
| DeepMind.google | July 16, 2026
Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs outline joint strategies to boost global bioresilience, including advances like AlphaFold, IsoDDE, and AlphaGenome. Partnerships with governments and biosecurity groups aim to prevent model misuse, enable rapid pathogen detection, and accelerate vaccine or drug design, all supported by AI-driven processes for prevention, detection, and response.
36.)
Create, Edit and Star in Videos With Two Google Vids Updates
| Blog.Google.com | July 16, 2026
Google Vids adds Gemini Omni, enabling users to generate and edit high-quality videos using plain-language prompts and image references. A new personal avatar feature lets users create a digital likeness that can deliver messages with just a selfie and voice sample, bypassing the need for camera recordings. All AI-generated clips include invisible SynthID watermarks for transparency.
37.)
NotebookLM Is Now Gemini Notebook
| Blog.Google.com | July 16, 2026
Google has rebranded NotebookLM as Gemini Notebook, reflecting deeper integration across the Google ecosystem. The update introduces the ability to run and execute code natively within notebooks and expands syncing between Gemini app, Google Search, and the standalone experience. Josh Woodward, VP at Google Labs, confirmed rollouts for Pro and Workspace AI Ultra users.
38.)
Automations in Grok
| X.AI.com | July 16, 2026
X.AI introduces Automations for Grok, allowing users to schedule or trigger jobs based on specific criteria such as email arrivals or custom schedules. Instructions read like chat messages and can include file attachments, connectors, and skills. Scheduled automations are open to all, while email-triggered workflows require SuperGrok.
39.)
Claude Can Now Use Your 1Password Credentials for You
| TheVerge.com | July 16, 2026
Anthropic’s Claude can now use 1Password credentials to automate multi-step tasks like booking travel and managing online accounts, thanks to a new browser integration. Using a zero-exposure security framework, Claude accesses only specific credentials without seeing passwords or MFA codes. The feature is available for Mac users on 1Password’s business, family, and individual plans.
40.)
Moonshot’s Upcoming Kimi 3 Is Expected to Close the Gap With Anthropic’s Opus 4.8
| TechCrunch.com | July 16, 2026
Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3, with an estimated 2-3 trillion parameters, is poised for release and may match or exceed Anthropic’s Opus 4.8, according to the Financial Times. The move comes as Moonshot raises fresh capital at a potential $31.5 billion valuation, amid broader debate over open-weight versus closed-source AI models for enterprise use.
41.)
Connect More of Your Apps to Search
| Blog.Google.com | July 16, 2026
Google is rolling out new integrations in Search’s AI Mode that let users securely connect third-party apps like Instacart, Canva, and YouTube Music. Chips Mistry, Senior Product Manager, and Biharck Araujo, Engineering Lead, detail how users can now add groceries, create design projects, or build playlists directly from the search interface without navigating away.
42.)
Build Without Limits on Roblox
| AboutRoblox.com | July 16, 2026
Roblox announced Build, a mobile-first AI creation tab within its app, letting users create basic games from text prompts and iterate via Studio. Public alpha testing begins July 28 in New Zealand, with a base-level version free and paid upgrades for advanced features. Upcoming tools include analytics, playtesting, and procedural 3D model generation.
43.)
Kimi K3: Open Frontier Intelligence
| Kimi.com | July 16, 2026
Kimi has introduced Kimi K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-source AI model built with Delta Attention and Attention Residuals, offering native vision capabilities and a 1-million-token context window. Benchmark results show Kimi K3 surpasses most open alternatives in long-horizon coding, knowledge work, and multimodal reasoning, though it still trails Claude Fable 5 and GPT 5.6 Sol overall. Full model weights will be released by July 27, 2026, with API pricing at $0.30/MTok for cache-hit input.
44.)
How Shopify Uses ChatGPT Work To Build Faster With AI Agents
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 16, 2026
Showcasing practical real-world advances, Chris Jones demonstrates how ChatGPT Work is helping Shopify teams streamline operations using AI agents. By minimizing engineering dependencies, Jones highlights tangible gains in speed and autonomy that are reshaping internal workflows.
45.)
ChatGPT Can Now Complete Tasks On Your Computer
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 16, 2026
Showcasing the new capabilities in GPT-5.6, OpenAI demonstrates how ChatGPT seamlessly interacts with local apps and browsers across both macOS and Windows. The walk-through highlights real-time collaboration, multi-step workflows, and ways the assistant blends into daily digital routines.
46.)
Demis Hassabis & Sergey Brin: Web’s Future, AGI Timeline, AI For Job Interviews, Simulation Theory
| Alex Kantrowitz | YouTube.com | July 16, 2026
Showcasing a headline conversation, Alex Kantrowitz presses DeepMind’s Demis Hassabis and Google co-founder Sergey Brin for rapid-fire takes on the web’s evolution, AGI’s timeline, and the ethics of AI in hiring. Their rapid exchanges reveal how leading technologists view the simulation hypothesis and the upcoming transformations in work.
47.)
What Racing Reveals About Working With AI — The OpenAI Podcast Ep. 22
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 16, 2026
Analytically breaking down the intersection of racing and machine intelligence, Joyce Ruffell uncovers how AI is shifting the balance of data, talent, and opportunity on and off the track. Joined by Chase Holden, Ruffell details collaborations with Chip Ganassi Racing and the ways ChatGPT and Codex are reshaping the advantage of smaller racing teams.
48.)
Claude Just Revealed AI’s Biggest Problem
| Two Minute Papers | YouTube.com | July 16, 2026
Cutting through the hype around AI assistants, Károly Zsolnai-Fehér examines what Claude’s latest research unveils about persistent challenges in coding support. Drawing on Anthropic’s newly released paper, he highlights why even the most advanced systems still hit surprising limitations and what that means for real-world productivity.
49.)
The AI Era Needs A Different Kind Of Talent | Peter Steinberger | OpenAI France
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 16, 2026
Analytically breaking down shifting recruitment demands, Peter Steinberger highlights how AI is changing the profile of an ideal tech hire. He argues that skills like creativity, adaptability, and collaborating with AI agents are taking center stage as organizations recalibrate their definition of top talent.
50.)
Think AI-First: Emmanuel Marill On Building AI-Native Companies | OpenAI France
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 16, 2026
In this analytical breakdown, Emmanuel Marill challenges conventional thinking on AI strategy by arguing for AI-native approaches instead of incremental add-ons. He highlights the importance of focus and solving business problems, while spotlighting France’s emergence as a global leader in AI innovation.
51.)
Stop Prompting. Start Giving AI Goals. | Katia Gil Guzman | OpenAI France
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 16, 2026
Episode after episode, Katia Gil Guzman explores why a shift from narrow AI prompting to broader goal-setting is taking hold. She unpacks how this new approach is empowering a diverse wave of builders in France to shape AI’s next chapter.
52.)
From AI Pilots To Production | François Weider On Enterprise Adoption | OpenAI France
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 16, 2026
Analytical in approach, François Weider breaks down how enterprises are moving AI projects from the pilot phase to full-scale deployment. He highlights the importance of focused use case selection and the role of executive leadership in ensuring AI generates real business outcomes.
53.)
What Great AI Founders Do Differently | Laura Modiano | OpenAI France
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 16, 2026
Analytical in her approach, Laura Modiano examines how Europe’s top AI founders distinguish themselves through rapid iteration and deep customer insight. Drawing from firsthand experiences at OpenAI France, she highlights the practical habits and decisions that consistently set successful startups apart.
54.)
Have A Crazy Idea? Build It With Codex | Romain Huet | OpenAI France
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 16, 2026
Analytical from the heart of OpenAI France, Romain Huet highlights how France’s thriving developer community is channeling curiosity into rapid innovation with tools like Codex. He emphasizes that the spark of inventiveness, when paired with the right technology, is fueling a new era of ambitious project building.
55.)
The 17-Year-Old Student Who Solved a Major Math Mystery
| Quanta Magazine | YouTube.com | July 16, 2026
Analytically tracing the journey of mathematical discovery, Quanta Magazine highlights Hannah Cairo’s pivotal role in upending a decades-old conjecture in Fourier restriction theory. The episode unpacks Cairo’s creative approach to the Mizohata-Takeuchi conjecture and explores the broader significance her breakthrough holds for the mathematics community.
56.)
Mattpocock/Skills: A Complete AI Coding Workflow, End-To-End
| Matt Pocock | YouTube.com | July 16, 2026
Showcasing his highly starred skills repository, Matt Pocock walks viewers through an AI-driven coding workflow from installation to code review. He details each stage, including documentation interviews, spec and ticket creation, and practical implementation, revealing how the entire process comes together seamlessly.
July 15, 2026 📅️
57.)
Google Wants an AI Referee
| TheNeuronDaily.com | July 15, 2026
Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is urging the US to create a national AI standards body modeled after FINRA to vet advanced models before launch. He proposes an independent board, industry-funded, with initial voluntary submissions moving to mandatory checks for frontier models. The move follows recent regulatory freezes and growing industry calls for AI oversight amid national security concerns.
58.)
Grok Build Is Now Open Source
| XAI.com | July 15, 2026
SpaceXAI has open-sourced Grok Build, its coding agent and terminal UI, making the codebase available on GitHub. The release includes the agent loop, code tools, terminal UI, and an extension system supporting skills, plugins, hooks, MCP servers, and subagents. Grok Build now supports fully local-first workflows and can be compiled and configured for local inference.
59.)
Perplexity AI
| PerplexityAI.com | July 15, 2026
Perplexity announced SPACE, a bespoke sandbox platform for its agentic Computer system, engineered to balance functionality, efficiency, and security. SPACE employs ephemeral microVM sandboxes, rolling snapshots, strict credential isolation, and node-level controls to protect sensitive data during long-running AI agent tasks. The system supports on-prem and offline deployments.
60.)
Inkling: Our Open-Weights Model
| ThinkingMachines.ai | July 15, 2026
Thinking Machines Lab launched Inkling, a Mixture-of-Experts transformer with 975B parameters (41B active) and 1M-token context, trained on 45T tokens across text, images, audio, and video. Full model weights are open-sourced on Hugging Face, with fine-tuning available via Tinker. Inkling ranks competitively on benchmarks like Terminal Bench 2.1 and FORTRESS, and a smaller 12B-parameter variant is coming soon.
61.)
Hack Reveals Suno AI Music Generator Scraped YouTube, Deezer, and Genius
| 404Media.co | July 15, 2026
A hacker reportedly breached Suno, one of the largest AI music generators, exposing source code and confirming the platform scraped millions of songs and lyrics from services like YouTube Music, Deezer, and Genius to train its models. Data show Suno ingested at least decades worth of music and podcasts, contradicting Suno’s fair use defense in copyright lawsuits.
62.)
OpenAI Finally Launches Hardware… for Codex
| TheVerge.com | July 15, 2026
OpenAI introduced Codex Micro, a $230 limited-edition macro pad created in partnership with Work Louder, designed to manage and monitor Codex coding agents.
